archetypes.name: The Waterboy
archetypes.shortDesc: System fallback — you probably don't even watch hoops.
archetypes.fullDesc: Congrats. The system flipped through 25 fan archetypes and none of them fit. You were probably dragged here by a friend, or you thought this was a link to an NBA 2K roster update. No hard feelings — somebody has to warm the end of the bench.
archetypes.quote: "Basketball? I thought this was a sneaker quiz."
archetypes.teamArchetype: The two-way contract guy who gets waived in training camp.
archetypes.triggerCondition: User vector's minimum Manhattan distance to all 25 standard archetypes > threshold 18

archetypes.name: The Six-Pack Spectator
archetypes.shortDesc: Hidden ending: the cold ones took over your fan arc.
archetypes.fullDesc: For you, basketball is just a socially acceptable reason to crack a beer. Tip-off? Crack one. TV timeout? Crack one. Slow-mo replay? Go ahead, crack one. By the third quarter you've lost track of the score, but you're absolutely certain tonight is a great night.
archetypes.quote: "I'm not here for the ball. I'm here to watch the ball while I drink."
archetypes.teamArchetype: Milwaukee Bucks. Milwaukee = Beer City. Destiny.
archetypes.triggerCondition: Q25 answer D (required) AND F1 ≥ M

archetypes.name: The GOAT Arbiter
archetypes.shortDesc: Reigning champion of the barbershop GOAT debate.
archetypes.fullDesc: Your entire sense of purpose hinges on proving "___ is the GOAT." Your bookmarks folder has 37 all-time rankings threads and you left a different top-10 under every one of them. You're either in a GOAT debate or on your way to a GOAT debate. Skip Bayless has a softer opinion than you.
archetypes.quote: "Your ranking is wrong. Here — let me fix it."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Depends on the day you ask — Monday it's Jordan, Wednesday LeBron, Friday Kobe, weekends TBD.

archetypes.name: The Brick Prophet
archetypes.shortDesc: The bigger the pre-game flag, the louder the post-game face-plant.
archetypes.fullDesc: You are the Westbrook of hot takes — loud before tip, humbled by the final buzzer. "We're winning by 30 tonight" — lost by 15. "This series is a sweep" — got reverse-swept. You never learn, because "next time it'll land for sure." Remember 0-for-27? That's your spirit stat line.
archetypes.quote: "Pre-game predictions are meant to get you clowned."
archetypes.playerArchetype: A supercut of iron. 2018 WCF Game 7 Rockets, 0-for-27 from three. Yeah, that one.

archetypes.name: The Process Truther
archetypes.shortDesc: Card-carrying disciple of Trust The Process.
archetypes.fullDesc: "Trust The Process" isn't a slogan, it's a theology. Team loses? "We need lottery odds." Team on a skid? "Top pick incoming, beautiful." Team wins? "NO — you just killed our draft position. This win is a historic disaster." You celebrate ping-pong balls harder than playoff wins.
archetypes.quote: "We trust the process. Losses are part of winning."
archetypes.teamArchetype: 2014-2016 Philadelphia 76ers — the patron saints of tanking.

archetypes.name: The Armchair Coach
archetypes.shortDesc: Draws up sets like Pop, couldn't cross up a traffic cone.
archetypes.fullDesc: You've never coached a game in your life, but you are quietly certain you'd do better than whoever's on the sideline. Timeouts are your showtime: "SUB HIM OUT!" "WHY AREN'T WE IN A 2-3?!" "Execution this bad, I've seen AAU teams sharper!" Your whiteboard diagrams slap. Actually running a huddle? You couldn't signal a timeout without fumbling it.
archetypes.quote: "Put me in — I mean as the coach."
archetypes.playerArchetype: You see Pop in the mirror. The league sees a mid-season interim getting fired by All-Star break.

archetypes.name: The Analytics Enforcer
archetypes.shortDesc: Wins every argument with a Basketball Reference screenshot.
archetypes.fullDesc: Your arsenal isn't trash talk, it's spreadsheets. You don't watch games with your eyes — you watch them through Basketball Reference. Your debate style is a screenshot: PER, BPM, VORP, WS/48, RAPTOR, EPM. The other side is still forming a sentence and you've already closed the thread. The only risk is you've forgotten basketball is a sport, not a regression.
archetypes.quote: "Don't tell me what you saw. Show me the number."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Your spirit animal isn't a player — it's Daryl Morey.

archetypes.name: Mr. 58-Point Benchmark
archetypes.shortDesc: Measures every blowout against Suns-Hornets, January 2009.
archetypes.fullDesc: You're a blowout pedant. Any lopsided score gets dragged to your measuring stick: "Only lost by 30? Not even half of a 58." "Won by 40? Still 18 shy of 58, nothing burger." Chris Paul can retire. 58 never retires.
archetypes.quote: "I'll never forget CP3's face that night." (You watched the box score the next morning.)
archetypes.playerArchetype: You are the 2009 Phoenix Suns — always on the side doing the curb-stomping, never the side on the floor.

archetypes.name: The Bandwagon Ace
archetypes.shortDesc: Whoever's winning the chip is your team this year.
archetypes.fullDesc: Your allegiance rotates with the Larry O'Brien. 2016 you were a Cavs fan. 2017 you flipped to Warriors. 2021 you were Bucks-in-six. 2023 you swore you'd been a Nuggets fan the whole time. People call you a fraud. You call it "embracing excellence." Your jersey drawer is suspiciously well-curated.
archetypes.quote: "I'm not chasing rings, I'm chasing the beauty of the game."
archetypes.playerArchetype: You are KD's spiritual descendant — he jumped to four teams, you jumped to fourteen.

archetypes.name: The Kiss of Death
archetypes.shortDesc: A walking reverse-jinx in clutch moments.
archetypes.fullDesc: Whenever it matters, you find a new way to cost your team. You tweet the wrong score, you show up courtside and watch the blowout, you buy the jersey of the guy who tears his ACL the next week. You are the anti-rally towel. Group chat has officially muted you in the fourth quarter.
archetypes.quote: "Not my fault — fate just picked me." (inbounds pass to the other team.gif)
archetypes.playerArchetype: J.R. Smith running out the clock in a tied NBA Finals game. Forever.

archetypes.name: The Hater in Chief
archetypes.shortDesc: Other team wins and you take it worse than their fans.
archetypes.fullDesc: Their ring hurts. Their comeback hurts. Their star's 50-burger hurts most of all. Your joy doesn't come from your team winning — it comes from the team you hate losing. You're technically a stan. You just stan the hate. Your KPIs are set by the enemy's box score.
archetypes.quote: "Wins? Look at their schedule first."
archetypes.playerArchetype: The star you hate most (you know his splits better than his real fans).

archetypes.name: The Ref Truther
archetypes.shortDesc: Every loss has a whistle-shaped reason.
archetypes.fullDesc: In your world, the NBA has never officiated a fair game. Wins are earned, losses are rigged. You slow-mo every 50/50 call and conclude: league + refs + sportsbooks are a three-headed cartel. You've emailed the league office 47 times. No reply.
archetypes.quote: "Hold on, let me go frame-by-frame — see? His toe's on the line. The league won't act so I will."
archetypes.playerArchetype: You are the self-appointed chair of the Citizens' Officiating Review Board.

archetypes.name: The Fire-Everyone Guy
archetypes.shortDesc: "Coach out! GM out! Owner sell the team!"
archetypes.fullDesc: Your entire solution-space has one verb: fire. Coach slumps? Fire him. GM drafts bad? Fire him. Star underperforms? Trade (fire, in contract form). Owner inert? Sell the team (the boss-level fire). Every post you've ever written can be summarized as "___ has to go."
archetypes.quote: "Three-game skid and the coach is STILL here? What are we waiting for, a sweep?"
archetypes.playerArchetype: You are the unofficial GM. None of your trade machines clear the cap.

archetypes.name: The Highlight Disciple
archetypes.shortDesc: Dunks only. Defense is a rumor.
archetypes.fullDesc: Pick-and-rolls, off-ball movement, defensive rotations? Never heard of them. You came for two things: dunks and blocks. No one takes flight for 48 minutes, you feel robbed. Your YouTube history is 100% mixtapes. You can distinguish a windmill, a tomahawk, and a free-throw-line launch down to the millisecond — but you don't know your starting point guard's first name.
archetypes.quote: "Defense? That's coach stuff. I'm here for the poster."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Vince Carter, Zach LaVine, Aaron Gordon — eternal dunk contest gods.

archetypes.name: The Logo-Shot Evangelist
archetypes.shortDesc: Curry hits = religion. Anyone else = luck.
archetypes.fullDesc: You preach the gospel of Stephen Curry. Any three-point make anywhere traces back to him: "See? Curry changed the whole league." What you live for is that 0.8 seconds the ball is in the air — you already know. You've copied his release form religiously. You convert at 1/10 the rate.
archetypes.quote: "That's not a three. When Curry takes it from there, it's a logo shot."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Steph Curry. End of list.

archetypes.name: The Buzzer-Beater Believer
archetypes.shortDesc: A hopeless romantic who never leaves the arena early.
archetypes.fullDesc: You are basketball's last romantic. You believe in comebacks, you believe in miracles, you believe in the last-second dagger. 30 seconds left, down 12, everyone else turned off the TV — you're still saying "35 seconds, 13 points, remember?" It's only happened once in NBA history. You're sure tonight is the second.
archetypes.quote: "Never underestimate the heart of a champion." (You know Dame said it too.)
archetypes.playerArchetype: T-Mac (35 seconds, 13 points) + Reggie Miller (8 points, 8.9 seconds) + every game-winner ever cut.

archetypes.name: The Ride-or-Die Martyr
archetypes.shortDesc: If the team folds, you go down with the ship.
archetypes.fullDesc: You and your team signed a soul contract. Penalty for breaking it: next lifetime. Team relocates? You move with them. Team rebrands? You rename your group chat. Team tanks 20 years? You've been there 20 years. When a friend asks why you don't switch, you look at them the way J.R. Smith looked at that pass — baffled but still in love.
archetypes.quote: "I'm not leaving. They need me." (glances at the 30-loss streak)
archetypes.teamArchetype: Die-hards for the Kings, Hornets, Pistons, or any perennially-cooked franchise.

archetypes.name: The Lone Wolf Spectator
archetypes.shortDesc: Watches alone, breaks it down alone, argues alone.
archetypes.fullDesc: You don't need company to watch hoops. You've got your own framework, immune to peer pressure. Someone says your guy's mid? You smile. Someone's flaming in the group chat? You lurk, then log off. You're the lone wolf of fandom — except unlike Luka, you can't even be bothered to yell at the refs.
archetypes.quote: "I watch alone. I enjoy it alone. Don't need anyone to co-sign."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Mamba mentality, west-coast edition — alone in the gym at 4am (you're alone watching 4am tip-offs on League Pass).

archetypes.name: The Stat-Padding Auditor
archetypes.shortDesc: "He's a stat-padder. You're a stat-padder. Everybody's a stat-padder."
archetypes.fullDesc: Your one great talent: pointing out that a player is stat-padding. Triple-double? Padded. Fifty-burger? Padded. "He's still in with a 20-point lead in the fourth — how is that not padding?" Every box score goes under your microscope — except your guy's. That's just "efficient scoring."
archetypes.quote: "Is this stat line real though? Open NBA.com, let me pull the garbage-time filter."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Westbrook's averaged-triple-double season — the original padding vs. production flame war.

archetypes.name: The 'Called It' Oracle
archetypes.shortDesc: Predictions land clean. Screenshots filed for later.
archetypes.fullDesc: Your hit rate on pre-game picks is suspicious. "We win by 7" — won by 8. "Series goes 4-2" — series went 4-2. You never screenshot your calls in advance because, you know, humility. But when someone else's take blows up, you always happen to drop a casual "told you."
archetypes.quote: "I called this weeks ago. Scroll up."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Paul the Octopus, but for basketball.

archetypes.name: The Finals-Only Fan
archetypes.shortDesc: Only visible during the Finals. Still asks "who's winning?"
archetypes.fullDesc: You appear in June like a cicada. Twenty minutes into Game 7 you post on Instagram: "who's winning?" with a random courtside photo you pulled off Google. You know players by name, not by position. That never stops you from tweeting "[champion] on top! Been here since day one!" the second the trophy is lifted.
archetypes.quote: "Is #23 LeBron or Jordan?"
archetypes.playerArchetype: You're the target audience for every Drake Instagram Story. Champagne Papi, champagne podium.

archetypes.name: The Vibes Captain
archetypes.shortDesc: Score is irrelevant. The story goes up either way.
archetypes.fullDesc: You don't watch for the game, you watch for the vibe. You're the Instagram story anchor, the group chat spam engine, the barbershop energy source. The ball could be on fire — what matters is the room. Your shared highlights are always three exclamation points and a caps-lock line.
archetypes.quote: "WE UP!!! WHO'S WATCHING!!! WE GOTTA GET THIS ONE!!!"
archetypes.playerArchetype: You're the NBA's hype man — no flip, plenty of shout.

archetypes.name: The Zen Spectator
archetypes.shortDesc: No highs, no lows, just hoops flowing through you.
archetypes.fullDesc: Win, lose, blowout, buzzer-beater — your emotional range is a flatline. Twenty years of basketball and the EKG never spikes. Someone asks "how'd they play last night?" You say "alright." Someone tells you your team got buzzered? "Mm." You're not numb. You've transcended. Mostly because your team hasn't won anything in two decades.
archetypes.quote: "Whatever happens, happens. I'll catch the next one."
archetypes.teamArchetype: Sacramento Kings fans pre-2023 — 16 straight years out of the playoffs teaches you things.

archetypes.name: The League Pass ATM
archetypes.shortDesc: Jerseys, kicks, rookie cards — if money equals love, you're a saint.
archetypes.fullDesc: Your love language is checkout. Authentic jersey? At least two per season (home and away). Sneakers? Your closet gave up. Rookie cards? You research Panini grading like Wall Street comps. Courtside once a year, minimum. Your fandom is fully quantified. Six digits, give or take.
archetypes.quote: "This isn't spending. It's investing in belief."
archetypes.playerArchetype: The NBA's favorite kind of fan: terminal consumer.

archetypes.name: The Pickup Legend
archetypes.shortDesc: "I got that shot" — followed by an airball.
archetypes.fullDesc: You don't just watch, you hoop. And you are convinced you could crack a G-League roster on a good week. On the blacktop you are "block Jordan" — step-back fade, post-ups, elite trash talk. Last three you made was three weeks ago, but that never stops you from yelling "I got that shot!" at the TV.
archetypes.quote: "I could hit that." (airballs it on the next trip)
archetypes.playerArchetype: The gap between your pickup self-image and your pickup game is bigger than the gap between Draymond's jumper and his confidence in it.

archetypes.name: The Team Mom
archetypes.shortDesc: Stresses over every player's knees. Own joy is optional.
archetypes.fullDesc: You don't sweat standings, you sweat soft tissue. "Oh no he rolled his ankle!" "Why's he looking thinner?" "Coach please cut his minutes, he's 22." You watch games the way parents watch field day. Under every injury tweet: "Take your time, rest up, seasons come back."
archetypes.quote: "He's TWENTY-TWO. Nobody put a deadline on the ring."
archetypes.playerArchetype: Every rookie's unofficial internet mom.

archetypes.name: The Whatever Fan
archetypes.shortDesc: "Whatever you wanna watch, I'm good."
archetypes.fullDesc: You are the purest form of "I'm good with anything." Which team? Whatever. Which game? Whatever. Who do you like? "They're all fine." Upside: no team means no playoff heartbreak. Downside: no team means no ring hysteria either. You are basketball's Switzerland.
archetypes.quote: "Pick whatever — I'll watch."
archetypes.playerArchetype: You are the undrafted free agent — not bad, just nothing screamed "take him."
